subject: Human Resources
title: Wage Theft
description: Learn Wage Theft in one minute.

hook.text: Wage theft steals more money from American workers annually than all robberies, burglaries, and auto thefts combined.

buildup.text: Wage theft includes unpaid overtime, minimum wage violations, stolen tips, off-the-clock work, and illegal deductions.

discovery.text: The Economic Policy Institute estimates employers steal $15 billion annually from workers through wage violations.

twist.text: Low-wage workers are most affected—those least able to fight back and most dependent on every dollar earned.

climax.text: Strong enforcement, worker education, and corporate accountability are all needed—wage theft thrives when nobody's watching.

punchline.text: The biggest theft in America doesn't involve masks. It involves paychecks.

quiz.question: How much do employers steal annually through wage violations?
